concepts.label: Information Theory
concepts.definition: Mathematical study of quantification, storage, and communication of information

concepts.label: Entropy (Information)
concepts.definition: Measure of uncertainty or randomness in information content

concepts.label: Signal Processing
concepts.definition: Analysis, modification, and synthesis of signals such as sound and images

concepts.label: Control Theory
concepts.definition: Mathematical framework for controlling dynamical systems with feedback

concepts.label: Feedback Loop
concepts.definition: System where output is routed back as input, enabling self-regulation

concepts.label: Chaos Theory
concepts.definition: Study of systems highly sensitive to initial conditions with deterministic but unpredictable behavior

concepts.label: Fractal
concepts.definition: Self-similar geometric pattern that repeats at every scale

concepts.label: Game Theory
concepts.definition: Mathematical study of strategic decision-making among rational agents

concepts.label: Nash Equilibrium
concepts.definition: Game state where no player benefits from unilaterally changing strategy

concepts.label: Complexity Theory
concepts.definition: Study of computational resources required to solve problems

concepts.label: NP-Completeness
concepts.definition: Class of problems where solutions are verifiable in polynomial time but no known polynomial-time algorithm exists

concepts.label: P vs NP
concepts.definition: Open question whether every problem whose solution is quickly verifiable is also quickly solvable

concepts.label: Automata Theory
concepts.definition: Study of abstract machines and the problems they can solve

concepts.label: Finite State Machine
concepts.definition: Computational model with finite number of states and transitions between them

concepts.label: Turing Machine
concepts.definition: Abstract machine manipulating symbols on a tape according to rules, defining computability

concepts.label: Church-Turing Thesis
concepts.definition: Hypothesis that any effectively calculable function can be computed by a Turing machine

concepts.label: Quantum Computing
concepts.definition: Computation using quantum-mechanical phenomena like superposition and entanglement

concepts.label: Qubit
concepts.definition: Quantum bit existing in superposition of 0 and 1 states simultaneously

concepts.label: Superposition
concepts.definition: Quantum principle where a system exists in multiple states simultaneously until measured

concepts.label: Decoherence
concepts.definition: Loss of quantum coherence when a system interacts with its environment

concepts.label: Bioinformatics
concepts.definition: Application of computational tools to analyze biological data

concepts.label: Sequence Alignment
concepts.definition: Method of arranging DNA/protein sequences to identify regions of similarity

concepts.label: Phylogenetics
concepts.definition: Study of evolutionary relationships among biological entities using molecular sequencing

concepts.label: Systems Biology
concepts.definition: Computational modeling of biological systems to understand emergent properties

concepts.label: Network Theory
concepts.definition: Study of graphs as representation of relations between discrete objects

concepts.label: Scale-Free Network
concepts.definition: Network whose degree distribution follows a power law, few hubs many nodes

concepts.label: Small World Network
concepts.definition: Network where most nodes are reachable in small number of steps

concepts.label: Emergence
concepts.definition: Complex system property arising from simpler interactions that cannot be predicted from parts alone

concepts.label: Cellular Automaton
concepts.definition: Discrete model with grid of cells evolving through simple rules producing complex behavior

concepts.label: Agent-Based Model
concepts.definition: Computational model simulating actions and interactions of autonomous agents

concepts.label: Monte Carlo Method
concepts.definition: Algorithm relying on repeated random sampling to obtain numerical results

concepts.label: Markov Chain
concepts.definition: Stochastic process where future state depends only on current state, not history

concepts.label: Hidden Markov Model
concepts.definition: Statistical model where the system is a Markov process with unobserved states

concepts.label: Principal Component Analysis
concepts.definition: Statistical method reducing dimensionality by finding directions of maximum variance

concepts.label: Clustering
concepts.definition: Unsupervised learning task of grouping similar data points together

concepts.label: Decision Tree
concepts.definition: Tree-structured model making decisions based on feature values at each node

concepts.label: Random Forest
concepts.definition: Ensemble of decision trees using bagging and feature randomness for robust predictions

concepts.label: Support Vector Machine
concepts.definition: Supervised learning model finding optimal hyperplane separating classes

concepts.label: Natural Language Processing
concepts.definition: Field of AI dealing with interaction between computers and human language

concepts.label: Computer Vision
concepts.definition: Field of AI enabling computers to interpret and understand visual information

concepts.label: Robotics
concepts.definition: Engineering discipline dealing with design, construction, and operation of robots

concepts.label: PID Controller
concepts.definition: Control loop mechanism using proportional, integral, and derivative terms

concepts.label: Kalman Filter
concepts.definition: Algorithm estimating state of a dynamic system from noisy measurements

concepts.label: Fast Fourier Transform
concepts.definition: Efficient algorithm computing the discrete Fourier transform in O(n log n)

concepts.label: Wavelet
concepts.definition: Mathematical function used for signal analysis providing time-frequency localization

concepts.label: Numerical Methods
concepts.definition: Algorithms for approximating solutions to mathematical problems

concepts.label: Finite Element Method
concepts.definition: Numerical technique solving partial differential equations by dividing domain into elements

concepts.label: Optimization
concepts.definition: Mathematical discipline finding best element from a set of alternatives

concepts.label: Heuristic
concepts.definition: Problem-solving approach using practical methods not guaranteed to be optimal

concepts.label: Metaheuristic
concepts.definition: Higher-level procedure for finding good solutions to optimization problems
